axis: What the peptide does at IL7R
ANTAGONIST hypothesis (immune evasion): Occupies the IL-7 site and BLOCKS IL-7 (competitive antagonist)
AGONIST hypothesis (IL-7 mimetic): Occupies the IL-7 site and TRIGGERS signaling (agonist / IL-7 mimetic)

ANTAGONIST hypothesis (immune evasion): Fewer/less-active T & B cells locally; dampened surveillance
AGONIST hypothesis (IL-7 mimetic): Expanded/maintained T & B cells; enhanced surveillance

ANTAGONIST hypothesis (immune evasion): Immune EVASION: quiet the local adaptive response, avoid clearance (fits commensal persistence)
AGONIST hypothesis (IL-7 mimetic): Harder to rationalize: why boost the immune system that targets you? (unless niche-engineering / tolerance)

axis: Epithelial axis (IL-7 also tunes IEC + microbiota)
ANTAGONIST hypothesis (immune evasion): Blocking IL-7/IL7R alters barrier + microbiota; documented to change colitis susceptibility
AGONIST hypothesis (IL-7 mimetic): Reinforcing IL-7 could support barrier homeostasis / a 'good-tenant' signal

ANTAGONIST hypothesis (immune evasion): Cytokine-blocking is the common microbial/viral immune-evasion tactic
AGONIST hypothesis (IL-7 mimetic): Engineered non-homologous IL-7R peptide AGONISTS exist (PMC10597491) - proves an agonist peptide is possible

ANTAGONIST hypothesis (immune evasion): Competitive occupancy alone; no need to recreate IL-7's productive geometry
AGONIST hypothesis (IL-7 mimetic): Must also recreate the receptor-dimerizing / productive conformation - a higher bar

ANTAGONIST hypothesis (immune evasion): STAT5-phosphorylation assay +/- IL-7: antagonist SUPPRESSES IL-7-driven pSTAT5
AGONIST hypothesis (IL-7 mimetic): Same assay: agonist DRIVES pSTAT5 without IL-7
